# HR / Payroll / Finance Survey — TimeHuddle Market Research

> Copy these questions into a Google Form. Each `---` is a new section break.

**Form title**: How Does Your Team Handle Timesheets and Payroll Data?
**Form description**: We're building a workforce tool and want to understand how
HR, payroll, and finance teams actually collect hours, process payroll, and get
visibility into team utilization. This takes about 5–7 minutes. No right or
wrong answers — we want honest impressions of what's painful.

---

## Section 1: About You

**Q1 — What best describes your role?**
*Multiple choice*
- HR generalist / HR manager
- Payroll specialist / payroll manager
- Finance / accounting
- Operations manager
- Business owner (handles HR/payroll yourself)
- Other: ___

**Q2 — How many employees or contractors do you manage timesheets or payroll
for?**
*Multiple choice*
- 1–10
- 11–25
- 26–100
- 101–500
- More than 500

**Q3 — What payroll system do you use?**
*Multiple choice — pick all that apply*
- ADP (RUN, Workforce Now, or other ADP product)
- Gusto
- Paychex
- QuickBooks Payroll
- Rippling
- Manual / spreadsheet
- Other: ___

---

## Section 2: Timesheet Collection

**Q4 — How do employees currently submit their hours?**
*Multiple choice — pick all that apply*
- A dedicated time tracking tool (e.g. Harvest, Toggl, Clockify)
- Our HR/payroll system's built-in timesheet module
- Spreadsheets submitted by email
- Paper timesheets
- We don't formally track hours
- Other: ___

**Q5 — Walk us through how hours get from an employee's time tracking to your
payroll system. What steps are involved?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q6 — How much time do you spend each pay period collecting, cleaning, and
entering timesheet data?**
*Multiple choice*
- Less than 30 minutes
- 30 minutes to 2 hours
- 2–5 hours
- More than 5 hours

**Q7 — What's the most error-prone or frustrating part of your current
timesheet → payroll process?**
*Long text — open ended*

---

## Section 3: Time Off and Availability

**Q8 — How do employees request and record time off?**
*Multiple choice — pick all that apply*
- A dedicated HR tool (e.g. BambooHR, Rippling, Gusto PTO module)
- Email or Slack request to a manager
- Shared calendar (Google Calendar, Outlook)
- Spreadsheet
- No formal system
- Other: ___

**Q9 — When an employee takes time off, how does that information reach the
payroll system?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q10 — How often are payroll errors caused by time off not being recorded
correctly?**
*Multiple choice*
- Frequently (almost every pay period)
- Occasionally (a few times a year)
- Rarely
- Never

---

## Section 4: Visibility and Reporting

**Q11 — Do you have visibility into team utilization — who is working on what,
how many hours per week?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — real-time or near-real-time
- Yes — after the pay period closes
- Somewhat — I have to pull it manually
- No visibility

**Q12 — What reports do you regularly produce from timesheet or HR data?**
*Long text — open ended (e.g. hours by department, overtime report, PTO
balances)*

**Q13 — If you could export payroll-ready hours directly from the time tracking
tool — no re-entry, formatted for ADP or your payroll system — how much time
would that save you each pay period?**
*Multiple choice*
- It wouldn't save much — our current process is already smooth
- 30 minutes to 1 hour
- 1–3 hours
- More than 3 hours

---

## Section 5: Systems and Integration

**Q14 — Do any of your current tools talk to each other automatically (e.g. time
tracking syncs to payroll, HRIS syncs to directory)?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — most of our systems are integrated
- Some integrations, but lots of manual steps remain
- Very little — most things are manual
- No integrations at all

**Q15 — If a new tool required employees to clock in/out to generate payroll
hours, how likely are employees to actually use it consistently?**
*Scale: 1 (very unlikely) → 5 (very likely)*

**Q16 — What would make you trust a time tracking tool's data enough to use it
directly for payroll without manual review?**
*Long text — open ended*

---

## Section 6: Google Workspace / SSO

**Q17 — Does your organization use Google Workspace (formerly G Suite) or
Microsoft 365?**
*Multiple choice*
- Google Workspace
- Microsoft 365
- Both
- Neither
- Not sure

**Q18 — If a new HR/time tracking tool could provision and deprovision employee
access automatically based on your Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 directory —
would that be valuable to you?**
*Scale: 1 (not valuable) → 5 (extremely valuable)*

**Q19 — What's your biggest concern about connecting an HR tool to your company
directory?**
*Long text — open ended*

---

## Section 7: Anything Else

**Q20 — What single improvement to your current timesheet or payroll process
would save you the most time?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q21 — Any other thoughts you'd like to share?**
*Long text — optional*
